executiveSummary:
## Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

National Medicaid enrollment has stabilized at 74.9 million enrollees as of February 2026, following the conclusion of pandemic-era continuous enrollment provisions. Year-over-year enrollment declines are evident across all major states, ranging from minimal decreases in North Carolina (0.0%) to more substantial reductions in Michigan (-7.6%) and California (-6.8%). Texas experienced a moderate 3.8% enrollment decline, bringing total enrollment to approximately 4.0 million beneficiaries, positioning the state as the third-largest Medicaid program nationally behind California and New York. Fiscal year 2024 expenditure data reveals significant variation in per-enrollee costs across states, with the national average at $2,883. Texas maintains relatively efficient spending at $3,014 per enrollee, notably lower than comparable large states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Ohio ($3,584), though higher than Florida's exceptionally low $2,036 per enrollee.

Quality performance presents mixed results for Texas and peer states, with the national average quality score reaching 65.6 out of 100 points in 2024. Texas ranks 30th nationally with a quality score of 62, placing it in the moderate performance tier but below the national average. This performance gap is particularly notable given Texas's high managed care penetration rate of 93.3%, which exceeds the national average of 83.2% and suggests potential opportunities for quality improvement initiatives within existing managed care frameworks. Recent federal regulatory activity indicates heightened oversight focus, including temporary enrollment moratoria and enhanced scrutiny of state directed payments, which may impact operational flexibility and require increased compliance attention across all state programs in the coming fiscal year.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Analysis

Texas operates the nation's third-largest Medicaid program with 4.0 million enrollees, representing a substantial but constrained safety net in America's second most populous state. The program's enrollment declined 3.8% year-over-year, closely tracking the national unwinding trend and performing better than large-state peers like California (-6.8%) and Michigan (-7.6%). However, Texas's relatively modest enrollment of 4.0 million enrollees stands in stark contrast to California's 12.4 million, highlighting the impact of the state's decision not to expand Medicaid under the ACA. Among non-expansion states, Texas maintains the largest program, but its coverage gaps remain significant given the state's size and demographics.

From a fiscal and operational perspective, Texas demonstrates both efficiency and concerning quality outcomes. The state's per-enrollee spending of $3,014 sits just above the national average of $2,883 while remaining well below expansion states like Pennsylvania ($4,363) and Ohio ($3,584), suggesting relatively lean operations. Texas excels in managed care penetration at 93.3%, significantly outpacing the national average of 83.2% and indicating strong program infrastructure. However, the state's quality score of 62/100 ranks a concerning 30th nationally, placing it in the bottom half despite substantial program scale. This performance lags behind southern peers like North Carolina and raises questions about care coordination and outcomes measurement. The robust managed care framework provides a foundation for improvement, but sustained focus on quality metrics and beneficiary outcomes will be critical as federal oversight intensifies and the program navigates post-pandemic enrollment stabilization.

alerts.title: High Per-Enrollee Spending in Pennsylvania
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 is 51% above the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost inefficiencies.

alerts.title: Massachusetts High Spending Despite Strong Quality
alerts.description: Massachusetts combines the highest quality score (81/100) with high per-enrollee spending ($4,221), suggesting premium care delivery model.

alerts.title: Florida's Unusually Low Per-Enrollee Spending
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,036 is 29% below national average despite being the 4th largest program, raising adequacy concerns.

alerts.title: Five States with Critical Quality Performance
alerts.description: Mississippi, Oklahoma, Alabama, South Carolina, and Arkansas have quality scores below 55, indicating urgent need for improvement.

alerts.title: Nationwide Enrollment Moratorium Active
alerts.description: Federal Register shows active temporary moratorium on enrollments across Medicare and Medicaid programs, impacting access.

executiveInsights.title: TX enrollments declining faster than national trend amid unwinding
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.8% YoY to 3.9M enrollees, steeper than national peers like NY (-2.2%) and PA (-2.9%). This signals accelerated disenrollment during the Medicaid unwinding process.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Rapid enrollment decline could strain HHSC operations and require immediate adjustments to eligibility processing, redetermination workflows, and managed care capacity planning.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag enrollment velocity concerns to HHSC eligibility team

executiveInsights.title: Multiple CMS moratoria on provider enrollment active this month
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register shows two separate nationwide temporary moratoria on Medicare/Medicaid provider enrollment announced May 15, 2026. These directly impact Texas provider network access.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Provider enrollment freezes could restrict HHSC's ability to expand network capacity and may require immediate policy adjustments or waiver requests to maintain adequate provider networks.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review provider enrollment moratorium impact with HHSC network team

executiveInsights.title: TX quality score lags significantly behind top-performing states
executiveInsights.summary: Texas ranks 30th nationally with 62/100 quality score, 17 points below national average (65.6) and 19 points behind top performer Massachusetts (81). This puts Texas in moderate performance tier.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Poor quality performance could trigger federal oversight, impact federal funding, and create reputational risks that affect contract renewals and expansion opportunities.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement priorities to HHSC quality team

executiveInsights.title: New CMS state directed payment regulations effective May 2026
executiveInsights.summary: Federal Register published new Medicaid managed care state directed payment rules on May 22, 2026. These regulations directly affect how Texas structures payments to managed care plans.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Regulatory changes could require immediate compliance updates to Texas managed care contracts and payment methodologies, potentially affecting 93.3% of Texas enrollees in managed care.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review new state directed payment rules with HHSC finance team

executiveInsights.title: TX per-enrollee costs exceed national average despite lower total spend
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $3,014 per enrollee versus national average of $2,883, ranking 4th nationally in total expenditures at $12.0B. Higher per-capita costs suggest potential efficiency opportunities.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Above-average per-enrollee spending indicates potential cost optimization opportunities that could improve budget performance and demonstrate value to HHSC leadership.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost efficiency benchmarks with HHSC finance leadership

riskOpportunity.label: Medicaid managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: Texas may recompete its $12B managed care contracts given high penetration rate and recent federal regulatory changes.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Engage HHSC early to understand recompete timeline and requirements.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality measure improvement
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks 30th nationally in quality scores, creating pressure for performance enhancement initiatives.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose quality improvement roadmap targeting top 15 state ranking.

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline response
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.8% YoY, requiring operational adjustments and cost optimization.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop enrollment stabilization strategy and right-size operational support.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory compliance
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple new CMS rules on state directed payments and provider enrollment create compliance requirements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory tracking and compliance implementation team.

riskOpportunity.label: HHS OIG oversight risk
riskOpportunity.description: Active OIG oversight of Medicaid programs poses audit and investigation risks for state operations.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Implement comprehensive compliance monitoring and documentation protocols.

riskOpportunity.label: Technology modernization opportunity
riskOpportunity.description: Texas HHSC procurement pipeline indicates potential for large-scale system modernization contracts.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor ESBD portal and position for MMIS and eligibility system opportunities.

riskOpportunity.label: Competitive market pressure
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple consulting firms targeting Texas Medicaid creates pricing pressure and capability differentiation challenges.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Strengthen unique value proposition through specialized Texas Medicaid expertise.

pulseMetrics.label: Total Enrollment
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 3,997,752 Medicaid enrollees as of February 2026.

pulseMetrics.label: Per Enrollee Spend
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas spent $3,014 per Medicaid enrollee in fiscal year 2024.

pulseMetrics.label: Managed Care Rate
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas has 93.3% managed care penetration with 3,863,808 MC enrollees.

pulseMetrics.label: Quality Score
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Texas ranks 30th of 50 states with a moderate quality score of 62.

pulseMetrics.label: Active Procurement Items
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Two active procurement opportunities identified for Texas HHSC.

pulseMetrics.label: OIG/Regulatory Signals
pulseMetrics.tooltip: Nine federal regulatory items and OIG oversight activities affecting Medicaid programs.

pulseMetrics.label: Next Milestone
pulseMetrics.tooltip: No specific milestone data available in the provided information.
